facts.text: Albert Einstein is closely associated with relativity and modern physics.
Albert Einstein's 1905 papers helped transform how scientists think about light, motion, and matter.
Albert Einstein famously said, 'Imagination is more important than knowledge.'
Einstein's work on the photoelectric effect earned him the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics.
Isaac Newton is closely associated with gravity, motion, and calculus.
Isaac Newton's three laws of motion became foundational to classical mechanics.
Isaac Newton wrote, 'If I have seen further it is by standing on the shoulders of giants.'
Newton's Principia is one of the most influential scientific works ever published.
Galileo Galilei is closely associated with telescopic astronomy and experimental physics.
Galileo used telescopes to observe Jupiter's moons, helping support the heliocentric view of the solar system.
Galileo is often credited with saying, 'All truths are easy to understand once they are discovered.'
Galileo is often called the father of observational astronomy.
Marie Curie is closely associated with radioactivity.
Marie Curie helped discover polonium and radium and pioneered research into radioactivity.
Marie Curie said, 'Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.'
Marie Curie was the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields.
Charles Darwin is closely associated with evolution by natural selection.
Charles Darwin's On the Origin of Species reshaped biology by explaining evolution through natural selection.
Charles Darwin wrote, 'A man who dares to waste one hour of time has not discovered the value of life.'
Darwin's long voyage on HMS Beagle deeply influenced his scientific thinking.
Michael Faraday is closely associated with electromagnetism and electrochemistry.
Michael Faraday's experiments with electromagnetic induction helped lay the groundwork for electric motors and generators.
Michael Faraday said, 'Nothing is too wonderful to be true if it be consistent with the laws of nature.'
The farad, a unit of electrical capacitance, is named after Michael Faraday.
Rosalind Franklin is closely associated with DNA structure and X-ray crystallography.
Rosalind Franklin's X-ray diffraction work was crucial to understanding the double-helix structure of DNA.
Rosalind Franklin wrote, 'Science and everyday life cannot and should not be separated.'
Franklin also made important contributions to the study of viruses and coal chemistry.
Jane Goodall is closely associated with chimpanzee behavior and primatology.
Jane Goodall changed the study of animal behavior through her long-term observations of wild chimpanzees at Gombe.
Jane Goodall said, 'What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make.'
Goodall's work showed that chimpanzees make and use tools, a discovery that surprised many scientists.
Carl Sagan is closely associated with planetary science and science communication.
Carl Sagan helped popularize astronomy for the public through books, lectures, and the television series Cosmos.
Carl Sagan wrote, 'Somewhere, something incredible is waiting to be known.'
Sagan worked on planetary missions and helped shape scientific discussion about life elsewhere in the universe.
Stephen Hawking is closely associated with black holes and cosmology.
Stephen Hawking's work on black holes and radiation made him one of the most recognizable theoretical physicists in the world.
Stephen Hawking said, 'Look up at the stars and not down at your feet.'
A Brief History of Time brought complex cosmology to millions of general readers.
Richard Feynman is closely associated with quantum electrodynamics.
Richard Feynman was known for both major contributions to theoretical physics and his remarkable teaching style.
Richard Feynman said, 'I would rather have questions that can't be answered than answers that can't be questioned.'
Feynman diagrams became a standard visual tool for describing particle interactions.
Nicolaus Copernicus is closely associated with the heliocentric model of the solar system.
Nicolaus Copernicus argued that Earth and the other planets orbit the Sun, reshaping astronomy forever.
Nicolaus Copernicus wrote, 'To know that we know what we know, and that we do not know what we do not know, that is true knowledge.'
Copernicus's work became a major turning point in the Scientific Revolution.
Dmitri Mendeleev is closely associated with the periodic table.
Dmitri Mendeleev organized the periodic table in a way that helped predict the properties of elements not yet discovered.
Dmitri Mendeleev said, 'Work, look for peace and calm in work: you will find it nowhere else.'
Mendeleev's periodic arrangement remains central to chemistry education.
Rachel Carson is closely associated with ecology and environmental science.
Rachel Carson's Silent Spring helped launch the modern environmental movement.
Rachel Carson wrote, 'In every outthrust headland, in every curving beach, in every grain of sand there is the story of the earth.'
Carson showed how scientific writing could shape public policy and public awareness.
Louis Pasteur is closely associated with germ theory, vaccines, and microbiology.
Louis Pasteur's experiments helped establish germ theory and transform medicine and food safety.
Louis Pasteur said, 'Chance favors the prepared mind.'
Pasteurization is named for Louis Pasteur and remains widely used to make foods and drinks safer.
Albert Einstein helped transform modern physics through relativity and work on light and matter.
His name became almost synonymous with scientific genius in popular culture.
Albert Einstein is one of the standout topics people mention when discussing Science.
Any overview of Science usually includes Albert Einstein.
Albert Einstein helped define the legacy and reputation of Science.
Fans exploring Science are often encouraged to learn about Albert Einstein.
The story of Science is hard to explain without mentioning Albert Einstein.
Retrospectives and fan discussions about Science frequently return to Albert Einstein.
For many fans, Albert Einstein captures part of why Science stays so memorable.
Lists, articles, and guides about Science often highlight Albert Einstein.
